Analyser et communiquer les défis techniques liés aux concepts artistiques et de design.  Rechercher et développer des prototypes pour les pipelines artistiques et aider à donner vie aux idées de vos collègues.   Qualifications   Plus de 6 ans d'expérience dans l'industrie.  Connaissance approfondie de Unreal Engine 5.  Maîtrise de la création d'outils et de pipelines intégrés à l'éditeur (in-editor).  Capacité à collaborer et à bien travailler avec des équipes de diverses disciplines.  Prendre en charge des contenus/systèmes de qualité finale (shippable), économes en mémoire et performants.  Capacité à penser de manière créative (out-of-the-box) pour surmonter les défis techniques.  Capacité à s'auto-diriger et à faire preuve d'autonomie lorsque cela est requis.   C’est un plus   Connaissance pratique de HLSL ou GLSL.  Connaissance pratique des scripts Python ou d'un autre langage de programmation.  Capacité à créer des outils et des ressources procédurales.  Bonne connaissance d'une application de modélisation 3D et d'un éditeur d'images.  Expérience avec les plateformes de génération actuelle (Xbox Series X/S, PlayStation 5).  Expérience avec d'autres moteurs populaires ou propriétaires (Unity, etc.).  Fort équilibre entre l'esthétique créative/visuelle et les compétences techniques.   Notre engagement envers l’égalité des chances  Gearbox Studio Québec s’engage à offrir un environnement de travail inclusif, équitable et exempt de discrimination, reflétant la diversité du monde que nous aspirons à divertir.  En tant que studio soucieux de l’égalité des chances, nous ne pratiquons aucune discrimination fondée sur la race, la couleur, la religion, le sexe, la grossesse, l’orientation sexuelle, l’identité ou l’expression de genre, l’origine nationale, l’âge, l’handicap, l’information génétique ou tout autre statut protégé par la loi applicable.  Nous nous engageons également à offrir des accommodements raisonnables aux candidats et employés qualifiés pour leur permettre de participer pleinement au processus de recrutement, d’exercer leurs fonctions essentielles et d’accéder aux mêmes avantages et opportunités.  Si vous avez besoin d’un accommodement à tout moment du processus de candidature, n’hésitez pas à nous contacter.

title: Director of Data Engineering
description_text: Who We Are  At 2K, we create some of the most iconic and culture-shaping video games in entertainment, including NBA® 2K, one of the top-selling franchises in the world, and legendary titles like BioShock®, Borderlands®, Mafia, Sid Meier’s Civilization®, and XCOM®, as well as fan favorites WWE® 2K, TopSpin®, and PGA TOUR® 2K. We build unforgettable experiences by pushing the boundaries of creativity, authenticity and innovation across every genre.  Our portfolio is brought to life by some of the most influential game development studios in the world. Visual Concepts, Firaxis Games, Hangar 13, Cat Daddy Games, 31st Union, Cloud Chamber, Gearbox, HB Studios, and 2K SportsLab create world-class experiences across platforms.  But what truly powers 2K is our people.  We believe the best ideas come from teams that feel empowered, supported, and inspired. As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive workplace where people are encouraged to come as they are and do their best work.    What We Need  The Director of Data Engineering is a technical and strategic powerhouse responsible for architecting the data foundations that power 2K’s global ecosystem. This role oversees the engineering strategy, telemetry standards, and infrastructure framework that transforms raw player interactions into high-fidelity, actionable intelligence.  You will lead the evolution of our data platform, moving beyond traditional ETL toward a proactive, AI-integrated environment. As a natural "bridge builder," you will partner deeply with Studio leads and cross-functional executives to expand the remit of data engineering. Crucially, you will act as a steward of our resources, applying FinOps principles to ensure our infrastructure is high-performing, cost-efficient, and that every technical investment delivers clear business value—ensuring the "juice is always worth the squeeze."  What You’ll Do  Architect 2K’s Next-Gen Data Strategy   Lead a forward-looking data engineering vision that scales across Sports, Core, and R&D, prioritizing high-scale telemetry and proactive data modeling.  Transition 2K’s infrastructure toward AI-augmented data processing, leveraging tools like Snowflake Cortex or Databricks Genie to automate and optimize the data lifecycle.   Drive FinOps & Infrastructure Efficiency   Own the cloud infrastructure budget, implementing rigorous FinOps practices to monitor, manage, and optimize data processing costs.  Evaluate technical debt versus innovation, ensuring all platform scaling is economically sustainable and tied to measurable ROI.   Own the End-to-End Telemetry Lifecycle   Drive the strategy for telemetry design and implementation, ensuring an intimate connection between game-client events and downstream analytical value.  Establish rigorous telemetry standards that ensure high-signal, low-latency data capture across all titles and platforms.   Build the Platform for ML Ops & Proactive Modeling   Design and scale a dedicated ML Ops framework that allows Data Scientists to deploy, monitor, and iterate on AI models with industry-leading efficiency.  Champion the use of proactive modeling to predict player needs and business trends before they occur.   Expand Impact Through Strategic Partnerships   Act as a key relationship builder, creating strong alliances with Studio Engineering, DevOps, and Commercial leadership to align data roadmaps with product goals.  Navigate complex partner dynamics with diplomacy, building trust and expanding the Data Engineering team's influence across the broader organization.  Partner tightly with the Director of Product of 2K Data to define and deliver upon a modern and comprehensive data strategy.   Build, Lead & Develop a High-Performing Engineering Team   Hire, mentor, and grow a world-class team of data engineers, telemetry specialists, and ML platform engineers.  Foster a culture of technical rigor, financial accountability, and "bridge-building" collaboration.  Foster an internal culture of innovation and fearless technical thought leadership throughout the data teams   What Will Make You A Great Fit   Extensive experience in Data Engineering or Software Engineering, with a heavy emphasis on high-scale distributed systems and telemetry.  Proven Success leading or managing engineering teams in complex, multi-title environments with proven track record of promoting engineers in both title and breadth of technical expertise  Intimate knowledge of Telemetry: You understand the nuances of event-driven architecture and how to design schemas that capture the "heartbeat" of a product.  Experience in Streaming Telemetry and Feature Pipelines : Solid understanding and experience in delivering large-scale, global data streaming services with scalable and sustainable architecture to support real-time machine learning operations and features  FinOps Mentality: Proven ability to manage large-scale cloud budgets, optimizing for both performance and cost-efficiency.  Master Relationship Builder: You have a track record of turning "difficult" technical partners into advocates and successfully expanding your team's strategic remit.  Growth Mindset: Indexes toward learning through challenges and didactic learning patterns to bring about change at the individual and organizational levels.  Experience in Matrix-driven and Product-led Environments: Able to successfully operate and thrive in matrixed environments led by product strategies and roadmaps.  A Track Record of Operational Excellence: You have demonstrated experience upleveling global data operations and a deep understanding of SRE or DevOps principles. You don't just maintain systems; you build the guardrails, automation, and infrastructure needed to scale them reliably.   Bonus Points   Full-Lifecycle Data Science & ML Enablement: Comprehensive experience across the data science lifecycle, from exploratory data analysis and feature engineering to model deployment and monitoring.  Software Engineering Background: Strong experience in full-stack development or systems architecture.  Game Engine Expertise: Hands-on experience with Unreal Engine, Unity, or proprietary game engines, specifically regarding how they emit data.  AI Fluency: Experience with Snowflake Cortex, Databricks Genie, Langchain, Rust or similar AI-integrated data platforms.  Passion for games and a player-focused mindset.     As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to ensuring that qualified individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform their essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us if you need reasonable accommodation.  Please note that 2K Games and its studios never uses instant messaging apps or personal email accounts to contact prospective employees or conduct interviews and when emailing, only use 2K.com accounts.   #LI-Hybrid

title: DevOps Engineer
description_text: On-Call Requirement: Yes (Periodic Rotation)  THIS IS AN ONSITE POSITION in Austin, TX.  Who We Are  2K is headquartered in Novato, California and is a wholly owned label of Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc. (NASDAQ: TTWO). Founded in 2005, 2K Games is a global video game company, publishing titles developed by some of the most influential game development studios in the world. Our studios responsible for developing 2K’s portfolio of world-class games across multiple platforms, include Visual Concepts, Firaxis,               Hangar 13, CatDaddy, Cloud Chamber, 31st Union, HB Studios, and 2K SportsLab. Our portfolio of titles is expanding due to our global strategic plan, building and acquiring exciting studios whose content continues to inspire all of us! 2K publishes titles in today’s most popular gaming genres, including sports, shooters, action, role-playing, strategy, casual, and family entertainment.  Our team of engineers, marketers, artists, writers, data scientists, producers, thinkers and doers, are the professional publishing stewards of 2K’s portfolio currently includes several AAA, sports and entertainment brands, including global powerhouse NBA®️ 2K,  renowned BioShock®️, Borderlands®️, Mafia, Sid Meier’s Civilization®️ and XCOM®️ brands; popular WWE®️ 2K and WWE®️ SuperCard franchises, TopSpin 2K25, as well as the critically and commercially acclaimed PGA TOUR®️ 2K  At 2K, we pride ourselves on creating an inclusive work environment, which means encouraging our teams to Come as You Are and do your best work! We encourage ALL applicants to explore our global positions, even if they don’t meet every requirement for the role.  If you're interested in the job and think you have what it takes to work at 2K, we encourage you to apply!  What We Need  We are seeking a DevOps Engineer who can build, support, and improve infrastructure, CI/CD pipelines, and operational tooling that help teams deliver reliable services and better developer experiences. This role focuses on hands-on engineering, automation, and day-to-day operational excellence in partnership with other engineers and teams.  What You’ll Do   Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines, cloud infrastructure, and deployment automation.  Support the reliability, scalability, and performance of development and production environments.  Improve developer workflows through automation, tooling, and documentation.  Contribute to observability efforts, including monitoring, alerting, dashboards, and incident response.  Partner with software engineers and platform teams to troubleshoot issues and improve delivery processes.  Participate in on-call rotations and help resolve operational incidents.  Help maintain infrastructure standards, security practices, and operational runbooks.  Support cost-conscious engineering through efficient infrastructure usage and tagging practices.   What Will Make You a Great Fit   Demonstrated experience in DevOps, SRE, or Platform Engineering roles within an enterprise or multi-team environment.  Hands-on experience with production cloud infrastructure (AWS or GCP) and containerized/orchestration platforms (e.g., Kubernetes/EKS).  Experience with Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) workflows (e.g., Terraform) and managing environment-specific configurations.  Experience building or maintaining CI/CD pipelines using platforms such as GitHub Actions or Jenkins.  Demonstrated operational experience, including participating in on-call rotations, managing production incidents, and using observability tools (e.g., Datadog, Splunk, Grafana).  Familiarity with managing work via formal ticketing systems (e.g., Jira, ServiceNow) and cross-functional coordination.  A focus on service reliability, documentation (runbooks), and operational excellence.  Strong troubleshooting and analytical skills, with a proven ability to identify root causes and work collaboratively across diverse engineering teams.  Clear and effective communication skills, particularly in conveying technical information, documenting processes, and adapting to fast-paced environments.   Bonus Points   Experience supporting GitOps-based deployment workflows and tools such as ArgoCD.  Familiarity with cost optimization practices, including tagging strategies, rightsizing, and resource usage analysis.  Exposure to security-focused automation, such as secrets management, policy enforcement, or vulnerability/scanning workflows.  Experience writing scripts or lightweight tooling in languages such as Python, Bash, or Go. Please contact us if you need reasonable accommodation. title: Senior Rendering Engineer
description_text: Job Title    Senior Rendering Engineer      Department    Engineering      Location(s)    Brno, Prague (Czech Republic), or Brighton (UK)      Reports To    Lead Render Engineer      Job Type    Permanent        Who We Are  Hangar 13 is an internal 2K development studio that crafts unforgettable AAA gaming experiences across all major platforms . With studios in Brighton (UK), Brno, and Prague (CZ), we work as one team to bring to life narrative-rich games that keep players immersed. Each of our locations provides the opportunity to work with some of the most talented developers in the industry, on creatively challenging and exciting projects, in a diverse and welcoming environment.  Job Summary  Hangar 13 is looking for a seasoned Senior Rendering Engineer who enjoys a collaborative and creative work environment and is looking to push the boundaries of cutting-edge 3D graphics. In this role, you will bridge the gap between stock Unreal Engine 5 capabilities and the high-performance, photorealistic visual demands of our current AAA projects. Working in a globally distributed team alongside our Tech Art, Lighting, and Environment teams, you will research, implement, and optimize custom rendering techniques that empower artists to deliver their vision stably and performantly. This is an ideal position for an engineer who thrives on navigating large codebases, solving complex technical pipeline overlaps, and making bold engine-level changes to advance game technology.  Duties and Responsibilities   Advance, modify, and optimize Unreal Engine 5's stock rendering pipeline to meet strict performance and artistic demands.  Partner closely with the Lighting Team, Environment Art teams, and Tech Art/Shaders departments to bridge team overlaps, eliminate pipeline bottlenecks, and bring the creative vision of our games to life.  Research, develop, implement, and debug high-end, photorealistic graphics techniques applicable to characters and large-scale environments.  Efficiently draft and deliver clear technical designs to different audiences, skillfully filtering out unnecessary detail for non-technical stakeholders.  Identify technical and developmental risks/obstacles within the rendering pipeline and proactively generate solutions to overcome them.  Navigate, maintain, and safely improve within a massive, multi-platform codebase using advanced C++ and modern graphics APIs.  Collaborate closely across multiple studio locations, ensuring close alignment with the Render Lead and local engineering anchors between studio locations.   Essential Skills, Attributes and Experience   Industry Experience: Significant experience in computer games engineering, specifically focused on real-time computer graphics, shaders, and engine development.  Technical Mastery: Advanced knowledge of C++ and multi-threaded programming techniques.  Graphics: Strong knowledge of modern Real-Time Rendering techniques, 3D math, and linear algebra.  Language & Communication: English proficiency with exceptional communication and collaboration skills.  Work Style: Demonstrated ability to work both collaboratively within a highly integrated global team and self-sufficiently as an outcome-oriented problem solver.  Qualifications: Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent professional work experience.   Highly Desirable   Shipped Titles: Shipped one or more AAA game titles on multiple platforms.  Engine Specifics: Deep multi-platform development experience utilizing Unreal Engine 5 or highly customized proprietary engines.  Console Debugging: Hands-on familiarity with console profiling and debugging tools (e.g., RenderDoc, PIX, etc.).       Hangar 13  prides itself on the diversity of its team members, partners, and communities.  For this reason, we remain committed to providing equal employment opportunity in all aspects of the employment relationship, from recruitment and hiring through compensation, benefits, discipline and termination.  As an equal opportunity employer, we are also committed to ensuring that qualified individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation(s) to participate in the job application and/or interview process, to perform their essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment.  Please contact us if you need to request reasonable accommodation.  SECURITY NOTICE - We have recently been made aware of increasing occurrences of bad actors posing as company HR personnel to gain information from "potential candidates", in the form of job interviews and offers. These scams can be quite sophisticated and appear legitimate.  Please know that Hangar 13 and 2K never use instant messaging apps to contact prospective employees or to conduct interviews.  If you believe you have been a victim of such a scam, you may fill out a complaint form at https://complaint.ic3.gov/ and https://reportfraud.ftc.gov/ detailing as much as possible. We are taking these matters very seriously and apologize for any inconvenience.  #LI-Hybrid  #LI-JF1

title: Principal Technical Animator
description_text: Job Title    Principal Technical Animator (Gameplay)        Department    Technical Art      Location(s)    Brighton, Prague, Brno      Reports To    Associate Director Technical Animation      Job Type (Permanent, fixed term, internship)    Permanent        Job Summary  The Principal Technical Animator (Gameplay) will help define the technical vision, architectural framework, and long-term strategy for character animation integration across the studio. In this highly influential role, you will champion innovation, driving the adoption of cutting-edge technologies to ensure our games achieve world-class animation quality and responsiveness. You will serve as a technical authority on gameplay animation, solving the studio’s complex R&D challenges while collaborating with design, animation and engineering to establish future-proof pipelines. While not a people manager, you will inspire, mentor, and elevate the technical capabilities of the entire animation department.  Duties and Responsibilities   Architect and oversee the high-level design of gameplay animation systems, ensuring scalability, performance, and seamless integration across complex real-time systems.  Drive R&D initiatives to evaluate and implement cutting-edge animation technologies (e.g., advanced Motion Matching, machine learning/AI animation, next-gen physics).  Partner with Engineering and Tech Animation leadership to implement project-wide animation roadmaps and toolsets.  Solve deep, complex runtime bugs and drive animation optimisation efforts to meet strict performance and memory budgets.  Define and document technical standards, best practices, and pipeline workflows for the wider technical animation team.  Collaborate closely with Design and Animation leads to heavily iterate on and perfect 3C mechanics (character, camera, controls), maximizing game feel.  Actively mentor and upskill senior and mid-level technical animators through code/blueprint reviews and hands-on guidance.   Essential Skills, Attributes and Experience   Extensive proven experience architecting and integrating gameplay animation systems for AAA titles using Unreal Engine.  Master-level expertise in Unreal Engine’s animation suite (Anim Blueprints, Motion Matching, Control Rig, State Machines,  and Sequencer).  Deep technical understanding of low-level animation concepts, including runtime optimisation, memory management, and threading for animation.  Exceptional cross-discipline communication skills , with a proven track record of aligning Art, Design, and Engineering directors on complex technical visions.  Expertise in feature breakdown and scoping, with the ability to translate abstract creative pillars into robust, production-ready plans..  A profound understanding of gameplay mechanics , game feel, and the mathematical principles behind smooth character movement and physics.  A portfolio or reel demonstrating a history of solving highly complex, innovative technical animation challenges in shipped titles.   Highly Desirable   Strong proficiency in C++ for extending Unreal Engine’s animation system, alongside Python for pipeline tool automation.  Experience contributing to proprietary engine tools or custom modifications to Unreal's core animation code.  Production experience with advanced DCC tools , including Maya API, MotionBuilder, or Houdini for runtime or pipeline integration.  Background in traditional animation or motion capture data manipulation to better bridge the gap between art and code. title: Localization Language Lead - German
description_text: TITLE : Localization Language Lead German    Who We Are  At 2K, we create some of the most iconic and culture-shaping video games in entertainment, including NBA® 2K , one of the top-selling franchises in the world, and legendary titles like BioShock® , Borderlands® , Mafia , Sid Meier’s Civilization ® , and  XCOM® , as well as fan favorites WWE® 2K , TopSpin® , and PGA TOUR® 2K . We build unforgettable experiences by pushing the boundaries of creativity, authenticity and innovation across every genre.    Our portfolio is brought to life by some of the most influential game development studios in the world. Visual Concepts, Firaxis Games, Hangar 13, Cat Daddy Games, 31st Union, Cloud Chamber, Gearbox, HB Studios, and 2K SportsLab create world-class experiences across platforms.  But what truly powers 2K is our people.  We believe the best ideas come from teams that feel empowered, supported, and inspired. As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive workplace where people are encouraged to come as they are and do their best work.    What We Need  As part of our Global Loc 2.0 evolution , 2K is deepening its investment in language-specific leadership to improve the voice, cultural authenticity, and overall quality of our localized content. We are seeking a Game Localization Lead to own the linguistic direction and execution strategy for all German content across 2K’s game portfolio.  This is an individual contributor role within the Content Localization (CLOC) team. You’ll serve as the key German language authority, working with minimal supervision across complex projects. You’ll apply deep linguistic and cultural expertise while partnering with developers, producers, QA, and publishing stakeholders to ensure language quality meets player expectations and platform-holder standards.    What You’ll Do   Own the localization strategy, quality bar, and final sign-off for German content across all 2K titles  Review and edit localized content (in-game text, UI, scripts, VO) for tone, clarity, fluency, and cultural relevance  Develop, maintain, and enforce glossaries, style guides , and cultural content policies for the German market  Collaborate with cross-functional teams—localization producers, embedded LQA, marketing, publishing, and dev—to guide language decisions and alignment  Serve as the main linguistic escalation point for German -related bugs, tone disputes, or QA challenges  Supervise and provide feedback to entry- and mid-level linguists or vendors working on German content  Consult with game studios and content owners on German age rating requirements , terminology usage, and visual/language compliance  Lead VO casting, script review, and direct creative sessions during German voiceover production  Regularly review in-development and near-final builds to audit for tone, consistency, and cultural resonance  Recommend and help implement process improvements that improve quality, reduce error rates, or optimize turnaround time for German localization  Participate in Global Loc 2.0 initiatives, working with senior leadership to shape evolving localization standards, practices, and tooling   What Will Make You A Great Fit   Professional localization experience in games, tech, or entertainment  Native fluency in German , with demonstrated mastery of tone, grammar, and regional nuance  Advanced English fluency (written and spoken); capable of representing language decisions across global teams  Proven ability to work independently on complex language projects , exercising judgment and creativity in ambiguous or conflicting situations  Strong working knowledge of platform-holder guidelines , naming conventions, and brand alignment principles  Comfortable overseeing or mentoring junior linguists and freelance vendors  Familiarity with localization tools (e.g., CAT tools , translation memory systems, XLOC, JIRA, CMS)  Deep understanding of German culture , media, and the German -speaking gaming audience  Experience with VO scripting, dubbing production , and linguistic performance direction is a plus  Bachelor's degree in translation, linguistics, media, or related field preferred   Why Join Us?  As a Game Localization Lead for German, you will directly shape how our players in  Germany and other German-speaking regions experience our games. You’ll own language strategy, guide voice and tone across titles, and play a key role in 2K’s broader effort to deliver best-in-class localized content through a modern, embedded, and scalable localization model. title: Concepteur Sonore / Sound Designer
description_text: English to Follow  Qui nous sommes  Gearbox Studio Québec rassemble une équipe de talents d’exception, animés par la passion de créer des jeux vidéo primés et emblématiques. Nos univers riches prennent vie à travers des récits transmédia, des expériences en direct et des produits dérivés, offrant aux joueurs des aventures immersives inoubliables.  Notre studio est reconnu pour son travail sur des franchises à succès comme Borderlands , Tiny Tina’s Wonderlands et Brothers in Arms , ainsi que pour le développement continu de licences cultes telles que Duke Nukem , Risk of Rain et Homeworld .  Gearbox a également des bureaux au Canada, dans les villes de Québec et Montréal. Gearbox Studio Québec est fier de faire partie de 2K, un label d’édition de Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc. (NASDAQ : TTWO)  Pour en savoir plus, visitez www.GearboxSoftware.com .  Ce que nous recherchons  Nous recherchons un concepteur sonore de niveau intermédiaire qui excelle dans la création d'expériences audio puissantes, immersives et tactiles. Dans ce rôle à temps plein, vous serez une force motrice derrière le feedback lors de combats, la mécanique des armes et l'environnement sonore de notre jeu. Vous ne suivrez pas simplement une fiche technique — vous aurez une voix déterminante dans notre direction audio, expérimentant des systèmes innovants pour rendre notre gameplay de tir incroyablement viscéral. Nous croyons en une grande autonomie et prise en main des dossiers; Vous serez un véritable acteur de l'identité sonore de notre projet et de la culture de notre studio.  Responsabilités    Création de bout en bout : Concevez, montez et implémentez des éléments audio de haute qualité (SFX, couches d'ambiance, interface utilisateur et audio cinématique) qui correspondent à la vision créative du jeu.    Intégration dans le moteur de développement : Intégrez l'audio directement dans UE5 en utilisant Wwise, assurant ainsi une performance optimale et une exécution technique.    Collaboration interdisciplinaire : Collaborer avec des programmeurs audio pour concevoir des systèmes interactifs, et coordonner avec les producteurs et les responsables pour la gestion des listes d'assets et les livrables.    Gestion de pipeline : Soyez votre propre responsable de livrer vos tâches audio personnelles, vos horaires et votre documentation technique.    Enregistrement sur le terrain et en studio : Capturez du matériel source unique via des sessions d'enregistrement en studio et sur place pour élargir notre bibliothèque propriétaire.    Qualifications    Expérience dans l'industrie : 3+ ans d'expérience professionnelle en audio dans le domaine du jeu vidéo, avec au moins un titre livré utilisant Wwise et un moteur de jeu commercial.    Maîtrise technique : Solide expérience pratique avec les DAW modernes (Reaper, Pro Tools, etc.), les plugins audio et les méthodologies d'implémentation audio interactive.    Connaissance du moteur : Bonne compréhension des flux de travail de UE5.    Écoute critique : Un portfolio diversifié démontrant une grande attention aux détails, de solides capacités de mixage et une compréhension approfondie de la narration sonore.    Contrôle de version : Familiarité avec les logiciels de gestion de versions (par exemple, Perforce).    Compétences interpersonnelles : Excellentes compétences en communication et un état d'esprit très collaboratif et axé sur la croissance.    C’est un plus    L'expérience en audio post-linéaire est un plus.    Connaissances de base en scripting ou expérience avancée avec UE5 Blueprint.    Gearbox Studio Québec s’engage à offrir un environnement de travail inclusif, équitable et exempt de discrimination, reflétant la diversité du monde que nous aspirons à divertir.  En tant que studio soucieux de l’égalité des chances, nous ne pratiquons aucune discrimination fondée sur la race, la couleur, la religion, le sexe, la grossesse, l’orientation sexuelle, l’identité ou l’expression de genre, l’origine nationale, l’âge, l’handicap, l’information génétique ou tout autre statut protégé par la loi applicable.  Nous nous engageons également à offrir des accommodements raisonnables aux candidats et employés qualifiés pour leur permettre de participer pleinement au processus de recrutement, d’exercer leurs fonctions essentielles et d’accéder aux mêmes avantages et opportunités.  Si vous avez besoin d’un accommodement à tout moment du processus de candidature, n’hésitez pas à nous contacter.  Who We Are  The Gearbox Entertainment Company is home to a world-class team that entertains the world with award-winning and genre-defining video games. Our games are entrenched with rich universes that are expanded upon via transmedia storytelling, live experiences, and consumer products. Gearbox Entertainment has become widely known for developing successful game franchises such as Borderlands , Tiny Tina’s Wonderlands , and Brothers in Arms , as well for continued development of acquired properties including Duke Nukem , Risk of Rain , and Homeworld .  The company was founded as Gearbox Software in 1999 by a team of industry veterans and is headquartered in Frisco, Texas. The Gearbox Entertainment Company is proud to be part of 2K, a publishing label of Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc. (NASDAQ: TTWO).  For more information, visit www.GearboxSoftware.com .  What We Are Looking For  We are looking for a Mid-Level Sound Designer who excels at creating powerful, immersive, and tactile audio experiences. In this full-time role, you will be a driving force behind our game’s combat feedback, weapon mechanics, and sonic environment. You won't just follow a spec sheet—you’ll have a defining voice in our audio direction, experimenting with innovative systems to make our shooter gameplay feel incredibly visceral. We believe in high autonomy and high ownership; you will be a true stakeholder in the sonic identity of our project and the culture of our studio.  Responsibilities    End-to-End Creation: Design, edit, and implement high-quality audio assets (SFX, ambient beds, UI, and cinematic audio) that align with the game's creative vision.    Engine Integration: Integrate audio directly into UE5 using Wwise, ensuring optimal performance and technical execution.    Cross-Discipline Collaboration: Partner with Audio Programmers to design interactive systems, and coordinate with producers and leads to manage asset lists and deliverables.    Pipeline Management: Own and track your personal audio tasks, schedules, and technical documentation.    Field & Studio Recording: Capture unique source material via studio and location field recording sessions to expand our proprietary library.    Qualifications    Industry Experience: 3+ years of professional game audio experience, with at least one shipped title using Wwise and a commercial game engine.    Technical Proficiency: Strong hands-on experience with modern DAWs (Reaper, Pro Tools, etc.), audio plugins, and interactive audio implementation methodologies.    Engine Knowledge: Solid understanding of UE5 workflows.    Critical Listening: A diverse portfolio demonstrating high attention to detail, strong mixing capabilities, and a deep understanding of sonic storytelling.    Version Control: Familiarity with source control software (e.g., Perforce).    Soft Skills: Excellent communication skills and a highly collaborative, growth-oriented mindset.    Nice-to-Haves    Experience in linear post audio is a plus.    Basic scripting knowledge or advanced UE5 Blueprint experience. title: Sr. Program Manager
description_text: About The Role:  We are looking for an outstanding individual to join our Program Management group as part of the 10X R&D team in Pleasanton .  You will work on Program teams with biologists, chemists, engineers, computational biologists, and software engineers who are developing truly new products. Our products have allowed researchers to study biology at unprecedented resolution and scale; yielding insights in diverse fields such as cancer biology, immunology, neuroscience, and developmental biology.  As a 10X Program manager, you will be on high-energy, cross-functional teams that are developing important new products, both instruments and consumables, for our customers. You will partner closely with the technical lead of each program to make sure that the priorities, risks, resources, and schedule are established, managed, and adjusted when necessary. You will use your solid technical background and prioritization skills to guide team conversations about the scope of the work being done and potential paths. Your strong communication skills will keep everyone on the same page and make sure everyone is heard. Of course, as a Program manager by nature, your excellent organizational skills will assure that nothing falls through the cracks or is missed.    What You Will Be Doing:   Serve as Program Manager on 10x R&D teams across new product development and introduction programs, leading cross-functional teams to define and commit to the right scope, schedule, and budget to deliver scientific and business results.  Partner closely with technical leads to establish and actively manage program priorities, risks, resources, and schedules — adjusting as the science and development landscape evolves.  Drive and track day-to-day program work, ensuring problems are rapidly identified and resolved using modern program management tools and techniques.  Lead program communications and phase approval reviews, providing clear and concise updates to all levels of the team and stakeholders, tailoring style to a broad range of audiences.  Actively leverage advanced AI tools (e.g., Claude, Gemini, Asana AI) to accelerate program planning, synthesize complex information, and establish best practices for AI-assisted program management across the group.  Assess and improve Program Management workflows and communication processes.  Note: this is not an IT or SW project management position   Minimum Requirements:   Bachelor's degree or higher in engineering, chemistry, biology, or a related life science or biomedical engineering field.  Proven experience (5+ years) in new product or technology development at a Life Sciences company, with a successful track record (2+ years) as the lead program manager.  Demonstrated ability to manage programs spanning both early exploratory research and late-stage development; comfortable with ambiguity early and rigor late.  Concrete cases of working with diverse stakeholders — biologists, engineers, chemists, computational scientists — to build shared plans and drive alignment.  Demonstrated adoption of advanced AI tools (such as Claude or AI-integrated platforms like Asana or equivalent) as a meaningful part of your day-to-day work.  Ability to clearly communicate complex technical topics across various levels of the organization.  Strong grasp of technical project management techniques for building and managing project schedules and project costs     Preferred Skills and Experience:   Experience developing platforms combining fluidics, optics, or molecular biology.  Familiarity with sequencing, imaging, or spatial biology workflows.  PMP Certification.  Hands-on experience with Life Sciences instrumentation.  Familiarity with existing 10x Genomics products.     #LI-MH1 #LI-Onsite   Below is the base pay range for this full-time position.  The actual base pay will depend on several factors unique to each candidate, including one’s skills, qualifications, and experience.  At 10x, base pay is also just one component of the Company’s total compensation package.  This role is also eligible for 10x’s equity grants, its comprehensive health and retirement benefit programs, and its annual bonus program or sales incentive program. During the hiring process, your 10x recruiter can share more about the Company’s total compensation package. Pay Range $151,100 — $204,500 USD About 10x Genomics  At 10x Genomics, accelerating our understanding of biology is more than a mission for us. It is a commitment. This is the century of biology, and the breakthroughs we make now have the potential to change the world. We enable scientists to advance their research, allowing them to address scientific questions they did not even know they could ask. Our tools have enabled fundamental discoveries across biology including cancer, immunology, and neuroscience. Our teams are empowered and encouraged to follow their passions, pursue new ideas, and perform at their best in an inclusive and dynamic environment. We know that behind every scientific breakthrough, there is a deep infrastructure of talented people driving the life sciences industry and making it possible for scientists and clinicians to make new strides. We are dedicated to finding the very best person for every aspect of our work because the innovations and discoveries that we enable together will lead to better technologies, better treatments, and a better future. Find out how you can make a 10x difference.  Individuals seeking employment at 10x Genomics are considered without regards to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, gender identity, or sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.  10x does not accept unsolicited applicants submitted by third-party recruiters or agencies. Any resume or application submitted to 10x without a vendor agreement in place will be considered unsolicited and property of 10x, and 10x will not pay a placement fee.  Please be aware of recruitment scams impersonating 10x Genomics. All recruiting communication will come from email addresses @ 10xgenomics.com . We also want to encourage you to apply to 10x Genomics positions directly on our careers site, Careers.10xgenomics.com or from reputable third party sites, such as LinkedIn or Indeed.  We will never request payment or sensitive personal information during the recruiting process.

title: Data Platform Engineer
description_text: Who We Are  2K is headquartered in Novato, California and is a wholly owned label of Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc. (NASDAQ: TTWO). Founded in 2005, 2K Games is a global video game company, publishing titles developed by some of the most influential game development studios in the world. Our studios responsible for developing 2K’s portfolio of world-class games across multiple platforms, include Visual Concepts, Firaxis, Hangar 13, CatDaddy, Cloud Chamber, 31st Union, HB Studios, and 2K SportsLab. Our portfolio of titles is expanding due to our global strategic plan, building and acquiring exciting studios whose content continues to inspire all of us! 2K publishes titles in today’s most popular gaming genres, including sports, shooters, action, role-playing, strategy, casual, and family entertainment.  Our team of engineers, marketers, artists, writers, data scientists, producers, thinkers and doers, are the professional publishing stewards of 2K’s portfolio currently includes several AAA, sports and entertainment brands, including global powerhouse NBA®️ 2K,  renowned BioShock®️, Borderlands®️, Mafia, Sid Meier’s Civilization®️ and XCOM®️ brands; popular WWE®️ 2K and WWE®️ SuperCard franchises, TopSpin 2K25, as well as the critically and commercially acclaimed PGA TOUR®️ 2K  At 2K, we pride ourselves on creating an inclusive work environment, which means encouraging our teams to Come as You Are and do your best work! We encourage ALL applicants to explore our global positions, even if they don’t meet every requirement for the role.  If you're interested in the job and think you have what it takes to work at 2K, we encourage you to apply!  What We Need  The Data Platform Engineer is responsible for designing and evolving the core architectural foundation of 2K’s data ecosystem. While Data Engineers build pipelines and DataOps Engineers manage reliability, the Data Platform Engineer focuses on building the underlying platforms—including Snowflake, Databricks, Kafka, and internal frameworks—that enable self-service data capabilities across the organization. this role ensures high-performance environment scalability for billions of global player events and creates seamless interoperability between complex data systems.  What You Will Do   Platform Architecture & Development: Lead the design, implementation, and optimization of core data platform components, including Snowflake, Databricks, and AWS S3 Data Lakes.  Streaming Infrastructure: Manage and scale high-throughput streaming systems using Kafka or Kinesis to support real-time game telemetry and low-latency analytics.  Self-Service Enablement: Develop "Data-as-a-Service" (DaaS) features that allow Business Units and Studios to onboard data and manage access with minimal friction.  Distributed Systems Optimization: Optimize Spark and PySpark applications for performance and cost-efficiency across large-scale distributed clusters.  Security & RBAC Architecture: Design and automate granular access control models to ensure data security and compliance with global regulations (e.g., GDPR, CCPA).  Interoperability & Standards: Ensure seamless data flow and interoperability between disparate platforms (utilizing technologies like Apache Iceberg) to maintain a Single Source of Truth (SSOT).  Technical Leadership: Serve as a subject matter expert for the data platform, translating complex business needs into scalable technical requirements and platform features.   Who We Think Will Be A Great Fit   Architectural Depth: Demonstrates a deep understanding of how platform components interact and scale within a global data ecosystem.  Innovation & Efficiency: Drives the adoption of new technologies and methodologies to improve platform performance and reduce operational overhead.  Problem Solving: Resolves complex, high-impact technical issues related to distributed systems and data availability.  Collaboration: Partners effectively with Data Scientists, Data Engineers, and Studio Leads to provide a high-quality developer experience.  Strategic Thinking: Anticipates future platform needs and aligns architectural decisions with long-term business goals.   Required Qualifications, Knowledge, and Job-Related Skills   Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field.  Experience: 6+ years of professional experience in data platform or distributed systems engineering in high-volume environments.  Core Technical Stack: Mastery of SQL and Python; expert knowledge of big data processing frameworks like Spark.  Cloud Ecosystem: Deep proficiency in AWS services (S3, EKS, IAM, SNS/SQS) and extensive experience with Snowflake or Databricks.  Orchestration: Experience building or extending workflow orchestration platforms like Apache Airflow.  Architecture Mindset: Strong understanding of data modeling methodologies (Kimball, Inmon, Data Vault) and how they apply to modern cloud data platforms.   Preferred Qualifications   Experience handling high-velocity telemetry from massive live-service ecosystems (e.g., gaming or high-volume consumer tech).  Experience with Infrastructure as Code (Terraform) for provisioning data stacks.  Knowledge of data observability and cataloging tools (e.g., Monte Carlo, DataHub, or Soda).  Experience with containerization (Docker, Kubernetes) in a data platform context.  AI Infrastructure: Experience building and scaling Vector Databases (e.g., Pinecone, Weaviate, or Milvus) to support Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).  Interoperability: Knowledge of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to enable secure, standardized data sharing between 2K’s data repositories and LLM applications.  LLM Data Tooling: Familiarity with data preparation and governance for fine-tuning Large Language Models.   What we offer you:   Great Company Culture . We pride ourselves as being one of the most creative and innovative places to work, creativity, innovation, efficiency, diversity and philanthropy are among the core tenets of our organization and are integral drivers of our continued success.  Growth : As a global entertainment company, we pride ourselves on creating environments where employees are encouraged to be themselves, inquisitive, collaborative and to grow within and around the company.  Work Hard, Enjoy Life. Our employees’ bond, blow-off steam, and flex some creative muscles – through corporate boot camp classes, company parties, our Office gaming spaces, game release events, monthly socials, and team challenges.  Benefits . Benefits include, but are not limited to; Discretionary bonus, Provident fund contributions, 1+5 medical insurance + top up options and access to Practo online Doctor consultation App, Employee assistance program, 3X CTC Life Assurance, 3X CTC Personal accident insurance, childcare services, 20 days holiday + statutory holidays,  Perks . Gym reimbursement up to INR1150 per month, wellbeing program with the chance to earn up to $93 per annum, charitable giving program, access to learning platforms, employee discount program’s plus free games and events! title: 3D Animator
description_text: HB Studios is a driving force in the creation of sports video games and the studio behind PGA TOUR 2K Golf – presently working on Console and PC. title: Senior Product Manager, Growth - Web & E-commerce
description_text: About the role  10x Genomics makes products that enable researchers to answer biology's hardest and most elusive questions. Our web experience is how the global research community discovers our products, evaluates them, and ultimately buys, and we are seeking a Senior Product Manager to lead the continuous evolution of that experience end-to-end.  This role is built for a product leader who blends strong web product craft and UX judgment with the technical fluency to shape how our site captures, structures, and hands off customer data to the systems behind it. You will partner closely with engineering and commercial leaders to launch intuitive, self-service web solutions that delight users and drive pipeline. You will also shape how the web platform connects into marketing automation platforms, CRM, CDPs, and the broader stack so that web behavior translates cleanly into qualified demand.  What You'll Be Doing   Own the product strategy for our customer-facing web platform, transforming complex scientific purchasing journeys into intuitive, self-service experiences across discovery, lead capture, quoting, and re-ordering.  Define how the web experience captures customer intent and identity, including forms, profiling, event instrumentation, consent and privacy controls, and lead scoring signals, so that high-fidelity lead and behavioral data flows cleanly into marketing automation, CRM, and the broader marketing and sales stack.  Lead the design and delivery of new web experiences alongside maturing existing ones, making sharp UX calls, partnering closely with design and engineering, and shipping a steady cadence of releases that move both new acquisition and existing customer expansion.  Build the connective tissue between web and the downstream stack, shaping lead routing, attribution, and data flow into the demand funnel.  Turn data into product judgment. Use qualitative research, web analytics, and quantitative metrics to validate ideas, shape roadmap priorities, and continuously improve conversion across the journey.  Act as the central alignment force between engineering and commercial leadership, translating complex business objectives into clear requirements, building consensus, and managing change across stakeholders.   Minimum Qualifications   Bachelor's Degree (or equivalent)  5 years of product management experience  3 years of experience shipping customer-facing web products, including lead capture and integration with marketing automation systems   Preferred skills and experience  This role requires breadth across web product craft, customer journey design, and the systems that sit behind the site. Here are the skills and experiences we are looking for, roughly in priority order:   Web Product Craft : Proven experience building and shipping customer-facing web experiences end-to-end. Strong UX instincts and a track record of making sharp product calls with design and engineering partners, grounded in customer research and a clear point of view on what good looks like.  Breadth of Web Product Experience: Track record across a range of customer-facing surfaces, including marketing sites, self-service flows, configurators, lead capture, and transactional experiences, with the judgment to know which patterns fit which problem.  Lead & Data Capture Fluency: Deep familiarity with how web experiences capture customer data and how that data is structured for downstream marketing and sales use. Comfortable designing capture experiences that meet global privacy and consent requirements without compromising data fidelity.  MarTech Fluency: Opinionated about the marketing stack (marketing automation platforms, CRM, CDPs, web analytics) and how web platforms integrate with it. You can hold your own with the engineers and shape the integration points that matter most to the web experience.  Iterative Delivery: Strong at scoping high-value MVPs and shipping incremental releases. You balance near-term experience improvements with the longer-term integrity of the platform.  Technical Acumen: Technical confidence to engage in robust discussions with engineers on web architecture, frontend trade-offs, instrumentation, and the data contracts between the site and downstream systems.  Cross-Functional Alignment: History of acting as the central alignment force between engineering and commercial organizations. You translate product and technical realities into compelling narratives, build trust, and manage change with marketing and sales stakeholders.  Below is the base pay range for this full-time position.  The actual base pay will depend on several factors unique to each candidate, including one’s skills, qualifications, and experience.  At 10x, base pay is also just one component of the Company’s total compensation package.  This role is also eligible for 10x’s equity grants, its comprehensive health and retirement benefit programs, and its annual bonus program or sales incentive program. During the hiring process, your 10x recruiter can share more about the Company’s total compensation package. Pay Range $164,100 — $222,100 USD About 10x Genomics  At 10x Genomics, accelerating our understanding of biology is more than a mission for us. It is a commitment. This is the century of biology, and the breakthroughs we make now have the potential to change the world. We enable scientists to advance their research, allowing them to address scientific questions they did not even know they could ask. Our tools have enabled fundamental discoveries across biology including cancer, immunology, and neuroscience. Our teams are empowered and encouraged to follow their passions, pursue new ideas, and perform at their best in an inclusive and dynamic environment. We know that behind every scientific breakthrough, there is a deep infrastructure of talented people driving the life sciences industry and making it possible for scientists and clinicians to make new strides. We are dedicated to finding the very best person for every aspect of our work because the innovations and discoveries that we enable together will lead to better technologies, better treatments, and a better future. Find out how you can make a 10x difference.  Individuals seeking employment at 10x Genomics are considered without regards to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, gender identity, or sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.  10x does not accept unsolicited applicants submitted by third-party recruiters or agencies. Any resume or application submitted to 10x without a vendor agreement in place will be considered unsolicited and property of 10x, and 10x will not pay a placement fee.  Please be aware of recruitment scams impersonating 10x Genomics. All recruiting communication will come from email addresses @ 10xgenomics.com . We also want to encourage you to apply to 10x Genomics positions directly on our careers site, Careers.10xgenomics.com or from reputable third party sites, such as LinkedIn or Indeed.  We will never request payment or sensitive personal information during the recruiting process.

title: Gameplay Animator
description_text: Who We Are  Founded in 2023, 2K Sports Lab is the newest stand alone studio at 2K looking to push game development boundaries to bring 2K's next groundbreaking sports franchise to life. We're building a world-class team at our new Vancouver, BC location where our goal is to deliver an exceptional experience through bold ideas, technical excellence, and creative collaboration. We're seeking passionate and driven professionals across all areas of game development to help shape this ambitious vision. If you’re interested in tackling unique challenges and think you have what it takes to level up our team, we encourage you to apply!  What We Need  Our studio is looking for an experienced Gameplay Animator (12-month contract) who has a high quality bar and a passion for replicating authentic sports behaviors. In this role, you will work closely with AI and Animation Engineers, Designers and Producers to implement authentic sports behaviors to build highly polished world – class gameplay for what will be the next massive sports franchise in our industry.  What You Will Do   Collaborate as a professional in a multidisciplinary game development environment  Participate in review/feedback process both in source animation and gameplay contexts.  Edit, enhance and clean up motion Capture Data in accordance with Gameplay and engine and design requirements  Collaborate with Design Leads to define the animation needs and create the design for animation based features  Collaborate with Engineers and Designers to influence the technical implementation of features in order to build the strongest feature possible  Collaborate with the Animation Director to understand, and deliver on, the quality bar for the animations that you create  Collaborate with the Design Lead and Engineering team to implement your features in engine  Champion animation quality and authenticity within your features, bringing the animation perspective to all decision making  Occasionally contribute elements such prototype assets, sketches, layouts and other elements for game/cinematic previsualization  Provide task estimations with regard to animation work.   Who We Think Will Be A Great Fit   A university degree in visual arts with specialization in graphic design, commercial art, graphic communications or cartooning or completion of a college diploma program in graphic arts is required  Experience or training in multimedia design at a post-secondary, college or technical institution may be required Please confirm if applicant has this experience  Creative ability and artistic talent, as demonstrated by a portfolio of work, are required for graphic designers and illustrators  A passion, and understanding of sports games  Experience working with ‘second-to-second’ interactive animation features  Player experience focused  Open minded, proactive and collaborative  Experience with Unreal Engine  Passionate about playing and/or watching sports  Passion for video games (not just ours!)  Subject matter expert in human movement pertaining to professional sport   The pay range for this position in British Columbia at the start of employment is expected to be between CAD $60,700 and CAD $89,860 per Year. However, base pay offered is based on market location, and may vary further depending on individualized factors for job candidates, such as job-related knowledge, skills, experience, and other objective business considerations. Subject to those same considerations, the total compensation package for this position may also include other elements, including a bonus and/or equity awards and eligibility to participate in our 401(K) plan and Employee Stock Purchase Program. Regular, full-time employees are also eligible for a range of benefits at the Company, including: medical, dental, vision, and basic life insurance coverage; 14 paid holidays per calendar year; paid vacation time per calendar year (ranging from 15 to 25 days) or eligibility to participate in the Company's discretionary time off program; up to 10 paid sick days per calendar year; paid parental and compassionate leave; wellbeing programs for mental health and other wellness support; family planning support through Maven; commuter benefits; and reimbursements for fitness-related expenses.  As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to ensuring that qualified individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform their essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us if you need reasonable accommodation.  Please note that 2K Games and its studios never uses instant messaging apps or personal email accounts to contact prospective employees or conduct interviews, and when emailing, we only use 2K.com accounts.

title: Community Manager
description_text: 2K’s Cat Daddy studio, based in Kirkland, Washington, was started 25+ years ago by industry veterans and is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Take-Two Interactive within the 2K Label. The studio is a fun, dedicated family of incredibly hardworking game developers. We have shipped games on almost every genre and platform, with a current focus on mobile games.  What We Need:  We're looking for a Community Manager to run day-to-day player-facing efforts for one of our mobile titles. Reporting to community leadership, this role executes community programs across platforms, runs engagement initiatives, and helps maintain feedback loops between players and the development team. You'll help operate systems that keep communities safe and active, support development professionals with player context, and bring the player perspective into feature discussions. Success in this role means stronger player trust and steady contributions to community-driven features.  What You Will Do:   Community Support   Serve as an active member of the community team, executing initiatives that improve player satisfaction and retention.  Act as a day-to-day liaison between players and internal teams, gathering community feedback and surfacing actionable insights.  Maintain an authentic brand presence across community channels, keeping messaging aligned with product and marketing.   Player Engagement & Content   Run and grow community programs.  Manage campaigns and content calendars that drive interaction across social, Discord, Reddit, and more.  Help create shareable content such as AMAs, patch previews, and dev updates that drive engagement and advocacy.  Monitor trends and community behaviors, flagging shifts and recommending adjustments.   Community Health & Moderation   Apply and help refine moderation policies that keep environments safe and inclusive.  Support crisis communications and high-impact player issues, maintaining trust and transparency.  Track community sentiment, trust indicators, and escalation trends, reporting on changes over time.   Cross-Team Collaboration   Work with Product, Live Ops, and Marketing to keep feedback loops tight and community input integrated into features and campaigns.  Follow team workflows, tools, and reporting standards to keep output consistent across projects.  Bring the player voice into sprint planning, roadmap discussions, and milestone reviews so player feedback informs prioritization.   Measurement & Reporting   Track KPIs for community performance, including sentiment, engagement rate, content reach, and response times.  Share insights and recommendations with the team and studio leadership, using qualitative and quantitative data.  Maintain dashboards and retrospectives that show the community's impact on retention, LTV, and engagement.    Who We Think Will Be A Great Fit:   3+ years in community management, running programs and growing communities.  Experience influencing game development with player feedback, working with product teams to get community-driven features on the roadmap.  Strong written communication, drafting player-facing communications with sensitivity and brand alignment.  Platform fluency (Discord, social, Reddit) with experience growing communities and using moderation and reporting tools.  Comfort with community metrics and reporting, turning sentiment into insights for dev, product, and marketing teams.  Experience supporting influencer programs and creator relationships that move product KPIs.  Familiarity with mobile live-service monetization cycles and content planning.  Experience working with IP licensors and global stakeholders is a plus.  Love for the NBA.   At Cat Daddy, we believe diversity of experience is fundamental to crafting accessible and fun gaming experiences for all of our players. We encourage everyone to consider being a part of our team.
